item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can see it now, everyone wanting to add to the list to share their opinions on what various movies should taste like. Hmmm, what movie would resemble lemonade?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Westhead is haping up to be a very screwed up and intriguing group. That detail on their expulsion gives a pretty good sign that they have done something very, very wrong.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure what you mean by 'the purpose' of it, but in-universe it's basically an anomalous drug being distributed by Westhead for the purposes of making money. The cognitive breakdown is a side-effect that they just don't care to fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. It's short and easy to understand, I just don't understand the purpose. This applies to most newly created SCPs I read nowadays, so maybe it's just a trend with the foundation that I haven't picked up on. there just doesn't seem to be any actual point, it just feels random. It's like "SCP-XXXX is a paperclip that transfers the consciousness of people from Czechloslovakia into various rocks made of Beryllium compounds. Oh, and, if you mine through the following gigabyte of various addendums and files, you'll be able to understand that this SCP is actually a world destroying horror that eats babies." (this is generally how I feel about most SCPs that are created). It's great that you came up with this idea, I just don't see why I should care about it when nothing actually stands out to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Operation Ebert

Heh.

I'm not sure how I feel about this one. I like that its short and sweet, and its very consistent in its concept, but I can't say it's really doing it for me. I don't feel any sort of strong emotion to this article one way or the other.

No-vote, for now. I'll give it some time to think on it.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to weryllium, CadaverCommander and LordofLaugh for their feedback in chat!
